_case: Sydney Desalination Plant (Kurnell, NSW) — offshore Tasman-shelf submerged multiport brine diffuser. CALIBRATION: the near-field return-dilution coefficient is FITTED to MEASURED in-class field data (nf_dilution_cal=0.871 -> field S_r=1.39*Fr, against the quiescent-laboratory 1.6*Fr of Roberts et al. 1997), from the Gold Coast Desalination Plant offshore multiport diffuser, Case 3-1 (100% capacity, measured 48.4:1 at the 60 m mixing-zone boundary) — Baum (2019) PhD thesis, Univ. of Queensland, Tables 2.2-2.3; peer-reviewed as Baum, Gibbes, Grinham, Albert, Fisher & Gale (2018), J. Hydraul. Eng. 144(11), doi:10.1061/(ASCE)HY.1943-7900.0001524. The FAR FIELD is NOT calibrated: farfield_disp_cal is unidentifiable from mixing-zone data (a 4x sweep moves the modelled dilution <3.5%; that station is near-field-dominated) and stays at its physical default of 1.0. NOT demonstrably conservative: across the four measured Gold Coast cases the model's dilution error spans 0.35x-3.4x. VALIDATED at the numerics/PDE level (lock-exchange front Froude number; 13/13 invariants). A prior revision calibrated against a synthetic transect constructed to be reproducible without tuning — that was circular and has been deleted. Grid/ensemble/t_end below are the values that produced case_study/outputs/.
_runlength_note: t_end=600 s with bottom_drag ON. The former far-field eddy-viscosity railing (nut->nut_max past ~400-600 s, which previously forced t_end=200 s and bottom_drag OFF) is RESOLVED by the turbulent length-scale limiter (strat_length_limit; Galperin 1988 buoyancy limit + geometric mixing-length cap) plus the semi-implicit k-eps sink. Confirmed on this grid: nut_cap_fraction=0% at t=900 s with bottom_drag ON (was ~29% at t=600 s before the fix). With bottom drag the horizontal reach is now BOUNDED and statistically stationary (oscillates ~40-54 m with tidal/stochastic breathing) instead of the pre-fix monotonic climb 70->96 m; it is reported as a central estimate, not a lower bound. The seabed footprint that governs compliance is near-converged (steady-window drift ~6%).
_provenance_public: Public design basis: SDP Kurnell, product capacity 250 ML/day (expandable to 500 ML/day), SWRO recovery ~47%, brine returned through an offshore diffuser on the open Tasman Sea shelf in ~25 m water via tunnelled risers with inclined multiport rosette heads. Ambient open-ocean salinity ~35.4-35.6 psu; summer thermocline ~18-21 C. NSW mixing-zone practice limits the salinity anomaly to ~1 ppt above ambient at the mixing-zone boundary.
_assumptions_engineering: Per-port nozzle geometry and the concentrate split are a REPRESENTATIVE deep-diffuser configuration consistent with the public capacity/recovery and a 60-deg inclined-dense-jet design, NOT measured per-port data. Mass balance: feed = 250/0.47 = 532 ML/day, concentrate = 282 ML/day = 3.26 m3/s; concentrate salinity = 35.5/(1-0.47) ~= 67 psu. Per-port Q_d = 3.26/72 = 0.0453 m3/s, U_d ~= 4.0 m/s at d_p = 0.12 m. Site-specific bathymetry/CTD/ADCP/wave/wind decks in case_study/inputs/ (credible representative survey).
